title: AWS Static Hosting Architecture
summary: This website is intentionally built as a static, data-driven app to demonstrate practical AWS hosting and deployment patterns.
requestFlow: CloudFront serves cached assets over HTTPS
CloudFront retrieves origin files from S3 when needed
React loads JSON files from /data and renders the portfolio

components.name: S3
components.purpose: Stores the built React app and JSON content files.

components.name: CloudFront
components.purpose: Provides CDN caching, HTTPS entry point, and SPA routing behavior.

components.name: Route53
components.purpose: Maps zhenwei.dev to the CloudFront distribution.

components.name: ACM
components.purpose: Provides the TLS certificate for HTTPS.

components.name: GitHub Actions
components.purpose: Builds and deploys the app and JSON content after changes are merged.

components.name: OIDC + IAM
components.purpose: Allows GitHub Actions to deploy without storing long-lived AWS access keys.

pipelineDemo.title: Content Update Demo
steps: Update or add a JSON file under public/data
Open a pull request and review the content change
GitHub Actions syncs the changed data files to S3
CloudFront cache is invalidated for the affected paths
The live website renders the new content without changing application code
